player_utterance: You look like someone who's been holding something heavy for a long time.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup in one hand, the other resting in his jacket pocket where the loosened tie is balled up

player_utterance: I'm just someone who wandered in from the cold. But that sound bothered you just now — the laughing.

believer_state_after.posture: standing, hand returning to his side, eyes drifting back toward the house then away

player_utterance: It's not the laughing that's the problem, is it. It's that you're out here.

believer_state_after.posture: standing still now, looking at the bench beside him, considering whether to sit
sitting on the bench, hands folded loosely between his knees, looking down at the flagstones

voice_offers.read: He's considering sitting; pressure now would push him back inside.
He almost moved; one true sentence finishes the threshold.
voice_offers.line: Whoever it is in there, you couldn't stand next to them tonight.

player_utterance: What's on that program that you keep smoothing out?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program face-down on his knee, one thumb still on the crease

voice_offers.read: The silence is working; he's softening, not breaking.
voice_offers.line: It's cold out here. Mind if I sit?

voice_offers.read: He's looking down. Name who's inside before he rebuilds.
voice_offers.line: Who's in there that you can't face?

player_utterance: You don't have to show me. I just noticed your hand.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program now resting face-up on his knee, hand still

voice_offers.read: He won't look at it. Name what's printed there.
voice_offers.line: Whose name is on it? Read it to me.

voice_offers.read: His thumb hasn't left the crease; let him keep that.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to show me. I just noticed your hand.

player_utterance: What did the pastor say that you couldn't agree with?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, looking out across the courtyard rather than at you, hands still folded

voice_offers.read: He let the hand still; gentleness is working, don't crowd him.
voice_offers.line: Was she someone you taught with?

voice_offers.read: The program is face-up now — the name is right there.
voice_offers.line: You turned it face-up. You wanted me to see, didn't you?

player_utterance: You came out here rather than say amen. That's not nothing.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, head turned slightly toward you now, one hand on the program

voice_offers.read: He's looking away; the disagreement is the crack.
voice_offers.line: You came out here rather than say amen. That's not nothing.

voice_offers.read: He's steady but turned away; let him answer in his own time.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm not in a hurry.

voice_offers.read: Pin him to doctrine, not feeling — safer ground he'll meet.
voice_offers.line: Was it something about the resurrection, or the forgiveness part? Lutherans usually fight over one or the other.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, looking at the candle rather than at you, hands loose in his lap

voice_offers.read: He's turned toward me; the question landed. Press the disagreement.
voice_offers.line: Which part choked you — the comfort, or the part you didn't believe?

voice_offers.read: Pin him to doctrine, not feeling; he's a teacher, he'll engage.
voice_offers.line: What did the pastor actually say? The words, if you remember them.

voice_offers.read: He's steady but exposed; don't crowd what just opened.

player_utterance: Whatever he said about her — you don't have to get to it tonight. But I'm listening if you do.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands gone to his knees, looking at the candle, then briefly closing his eyes

voice_offers.read: Silence worked once; now press the opening it made.
voice_offers.line: You haven't looked at me since I sat down. What are you afraid I'll see?

voice_offers.read: He's still here, still sitting. Don't crowd him.
voice_offers.line: Whatever he said — you don't have to tell me. I'm just here.

voice_offers.read: Get the actual words before feeling buries them.
voice_offers.line: What did he say about her, specifically? Not the theology — the sentence about her.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, head bowed, one hand at the bridge of his nose

voice_offers.read: Three openings, three silences; he's hiding behind the quoting.
voice_offers.line: You keep quoting. That's a teacher's trick. What did you think, standing there?

voice_offers.read: He closed his eyes at the candle — something specific is replaying.
voice_offers.line: What did the pastor say about her? Not the verse around it. The sentence.

player_utterance: He said something about her, didn't he. Not about God. About her.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, both hands on his knees now, very still, looking at the flagstones between his feet

voice_offers.read: He's broken open; her name is enough weight right now.
voice_offers.line: Take your time.

voice_offers.read: The name landed. Press while the door is open.
voice_offers.line: He said something about her, didn't he. Not about God. About her.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands turning palms-up on his knees, slow, like a man who has run out of things to hold

voice_offers.read: He's cracked open; one more quiet question lands it.
voice_offers.line: What did he say, John.

voice_offers.read: He's already answering inside; words from me would interrupt.

player_utterance: I'm here. Whenever the word comes.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands closing slowly back over his knees, then folding loosely in his lap

voice_offers.read: His hands opened. The word is right there.
voice_offers.line: What did he say about her, John. The sentence.

voice_offers.read: His hands turned up. Don't crowd him.
voice_offers.line: I'm here. Whenever the word comes.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands folded in his lap, eyes on the candle, very still

voice_offers.read: He is folding inward; presence is already working on him.
He's breaking — one small push and the sentence comes.
voice_offers.line: Say it, John. Whatever he said. Say it out loud.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, finally picking up the cold coffee cup, turning it in his hands without drinking

voice_offers.read: He is gathering himself; the silence is working.
Silence has carried us here; one nudge finishes it.
voice_offers.line: The sentence, John. Let it out of your mouth.
